_comment2: Every field that can appear in a material_properties entry. All fields except 'block' (or 'tag') are optional.
_comment3: ousia-related fields (ousia_affinity, ousia_capacity, etc.) belong to the ousia mod — include them here freely, they are silently ignored by BlockPhysicsRegistry and will be picked up by the ousia registry when that mod is loaded.
_alt_identity: Use 'tag' instead of 'block' to apply to all members of a block tag:
_alt_identity_gas: Use 'gas' instead of 'block'/'tag' to override an MGE gas's chemistry instead of a block's physics — see the GAS PROPERTIES OVERRIDE example at the end of this file. A gas entry uses a completely different, much smaller field set (currently just ph_value) and never touches block physics; everything else in this file is meaningless on a gas entry and is silently ignored.
_core_note: These six fields replace the old thermal/insulation/ system. Always provide them for any block that participates in thermal simulation.
_thermal_mass_note: J/°C — energy absorbed before surface temperature rises. air~1, stone~50, cast_iron~80, water~200
_conductivity_note: W/(m·K) — intrinsic heat-flow rate through the material. copper~400, iron~80, stone~2, wood~0.15
_insulation_r_note: R-value per block thickness for wall-stack math. air~0, glass~0.1, stone~0.45, firebrick~0.85, wool~0.9
_dissipation_rate_note: Fractional heat loss to ambient per tick (0–1). Most solids ~0.03–0.08
_is_airtight_note: Seals gas exchange. Solid blocks true; slabs, grates, fences false
_porousness_note: Gas permeability fraction (0 = sealed, 1 = fully open)
_thickness_note: Full block = 1.0, slab = 0.5, pane = 0.08. Applied to insulation_r and thermal_mass in wall stacking
_elec_note: Responds to induction heaters. Metals and some minerals only
_thermal_crack_note: Feeds stress into the CrackPropagator when block temperature exceeds threshold. Omit both fields to disable thermal cracking for this block (metals, firebrick).
_tct_note: °C above ambient at which crack stress begins accumulating. glass~80, sandstone~150, granite~200, brick~300
_tcr_note: Crack intensity added per tick above threshold. glass~0.008 (brittle/fast), granite~0.002, brick~0.001
_emission_note: Present only if this block actively emits heat. Replaces the old thermal/heat_sources/ entry for blocks defined here. The two systems coexist — blocks only in heat_sources/ still work.

emission._peak_note: Equilibrium air temperature this source drives nearby air toward in open air
emission._watts_note: Heat output rate. firestarter~150, campfire~350, lava~800, blaze_burner~1000, arc_furnace~3000
emission._induction_note: Skips air heating, directly heats adjacent electrically_conductive blocks
emission._o2_note: mbar/tick consumed at full output. 0 = no O2 consumption
emission._co2_note: mbar/tick CO2 emitted as byproduct of combustion
emission._profile_note: constant | linear_ramp | bell_curve | stepped. Controls how output changes over time
emission._stress_note: °C above which this source begins injecting structural stress into adjacent blocks
emission._pressure_check_note: Whether this source triggers a StructuralStressField re-evaluation of neighbors each tick

_dynamic_note: For block entities whose temperature is read at runtime (Clockwork Kelvin ducts, crucibles, etc.). 'read_via' identifies the bridge. peak_celsius is omitted — the live value is read each tick.
_phase_note: Each on_X key is a shorthand for a PhaseTransition entry. Multiple can coexist. All temp values are °C.

on_sublimate._sublimate_note: Entire block immediately becomes gas — use thermal_offgas for gradual emission instead

on_heat_above.note: triggers at sustained temperature above threshold

on_pressure_exceed.note: triggers when structural compressive load exceeds this threshold — use alongside structural block

_offgas_note: Two distinct systems: thermal_offgas (temperature-triggered) and ambient_offgas (passive, condition-gated). Both write to MGE EnvironmentGrid. Use on_sublimate for instant full-block vaporization.

thermal_offgas._scale_note: If true, rate multiplies linearly with (current_temp - threshold) / 1000
thermal_offgas._sustained_note: After sustained_ticks ticks above sustained_above_celsius, block converts to sustained_result

ambient_offgas._stops_note: °C above which ambient off-gassing ceases. null = never stops

_structural_note: Omit this entire block for blocks with no custom structural behavior — they use vanilla Minecollapse tags (if any) and no Misanthrope tracking. Present only when you want explicit strength values, custom failure modes, or shockwave tuning.

structural._strength_note: All strength values in kPa. Reference: dirt~100, sandstone~20000, limestone~50000, granite~200000, brick~15000, steel~250000, glass~1000, ice~3000, wood_along_grain~40000
structural._compressive_note: Axial load — columns, floors, buried blocks
structural._shear_note: Lateral load — explosions, kinetic impacts, torsion from rotating shafts
structural._toughness_note: 0–1. Low = brittle, cracks sooner relative to failure point (glass~0.1, granite~0.5, steel~0.9)
structural._curve_note: Strength as a fraction of full at each temperature anchor. Linearly interpolated between points. Omit to use default (no weakening until ~1200°C)
structural._density_note: Used for column load calculation and VS2 mass. air~1.2, wood~600, stone~2400, iron~7800, lead~11300
structural._frame_note: Steel/timber beams, brick arches — load-bearing, distributes load laterally to load_transfer_range neighbors
structural._transfer_note: Blocks. Structural frames spread load this far laterally. Increase for long beams
structural._failure_note: CRUMBLE (drops item, no chain) | CAVE_IN (Minecollapse chain reaction) | FRAGMENT_VS2 (detach as physics body) | LATTICE_COLLAPSE (internal atomic implosion)
structural._fail_thresh_note: Fraction of strength at which failure fires. 1.0 = at the limit, 0.8 = fails before full load
structural._crack_thresh_note: Fraction at which CrackPropagator activates — visual cracks and sounds appear before failure
structural._fragment_note: FRAGMENT_VS2 only. Groups smaller than this crumble instead of becoming physics bodies
structural._frag_vel_note: Multiplier on the initial impulse applied to the new VS2 physics body at detachment
structural._cave_in_note: CAVE_IN only. -1 = use Minecollapse default radius

lattice_collapse._mode_note: IMPLODE (shrink animation → result_block) | BLACK_HOLE (shrink → spawn entity → air)
lattice_collapse._bhe_note: Entity ResourceLocation for BLACK_HOLE mode. e.g. 'somemod:black_hole'. If entity not loaded, falls back to IMPLODE
lattice_collapse._duration_note: Visual shrink duration before block is replaced. ~20 ticks = 1 second
lattice_collapse._chain_note: Whether this collapse can trigger neighboring blocks' failure checks

structural._blast_note: null = use vanilla getExplosionResistance(). Set explicitly to decouple from vanilla for shockwave attenuation
structural._sw_amp_note: Multiplier on incoming shockwave strength. >1 for hollow blocks that focus blast inward
structural._sw_abs_note: Fraction of shockwave energy absorbed rather than transmitted. packed_earth~0.6, rubber~0.8, stone~0.05

_pressure_note: Present only for blocks that participate in pressure-differential simulation (hull plates, windows, rubber, fabric, etc.). Omit entirely for blocks that should be treated as rigid with infinite pressure strength.

pressure._yield_note: All threshold values are mbar pressure differential (ΔP = |P_internal - P_external|). Reference archetypes: stone/concrete elasticYield=800 plastic=800 ultimate=1200 stages=1 (brittle); steel elasticYield=2000 plastic=4000 ultimate=8000 stages=3 pauseTicks=120; wood elasticYield=400 plastic=600 ultimate=800 stages=2 pauseTicks=40; rubber/slime elasticYield=5 plastic=20 ultimate=60 stages=4 inflatable=true; fabric/wool elasticYield=2 plastic=10 ultimate=30 stages=4 inflatable=true maxInflation=0.9; glass elasticYield=600 plastic=600 ultimate=620 stages=1 pauseTicks=0
pressure._elastic_yield_note: ΔP below which deformation is fully elastic (recoverable) — block deforms visually but returns to original shape when ΔP drops
pressure._plastic_yield_note: ΔP at which plastic deformation stages begin — block advances through deformation_stage_count stages, each separated by stage_pause_ticks
pressure._ultimate_note: ΔP at which the block fails outright and a breach_mode event fires
pressure._stages_note: Number of discrete plastic deformation stages [1, 4]. Each stage advances the block visually (deeper inward/outward warp) and emits a sound event
pressure._pause_note: Ticks between deformation stage advances while ΔP stays in the plastic region — the 'tension pause' that builds dread before the next crunch. 0 = instant stage progression
pressure._fatigue_note: Rate at which stress accumulates per tick in the elastic region as a fraction of elastic yield, used for fatigue cracking. 0.0 disables fatigue
pressure._inflatable_note: Whether this block can expand outward into adjacent air under positive ΔP (internal > external) before tearing. Only meaningful for flexible materials (rubber, slime, fabric, wool, membrane blocks)
pressure._max_inflation_note: Maximum outward expansion as a fraction of one block space [0, 1]. At 1.0 the block occupies the full adjacent cell. At this fraction the block tears if ΔP is still above ultimate
pressure._inflation_rate_note: Rate of inflation per tick per mbar above elastic yield: inflationAmount += inflationRatePerMbar × (ΔP - elasticYield)
pressure._breach_note: What happens when ultimate_strength_mbar is exceeded. CRUMBLE (block destroyed inward/outward, drops items) | VENT (block destroyed, gas/fluid rushes through breach, MGE EnvironmentGrid notified) | SHATTER (brittle instant failure — glass/ceramics, particles + sound) | IMPLODE (inward catastrophic collapse when compressive, triggers LATTICE_COLLAPSE) | TEAR (fabric/membrane rip, inflatable volume collapses, gas vents, drops item if repairable)
pressure._compression_note: Multiplier on ultimate_strength_mbar when block is in compression (external > internal). Stone/concrete are much stronger in compression than tension. Default 1.0 (symmetric); stone ~8.0
pressure._crack_threshold_note: Fraction of ultimate_strength_mbar at which crack sources are registered with CrackPropagator. Default 0.6

_phasing_note: Reimplemented internally against FullStop's design (not the fullstop:phaseable/engulfing tags — those still run unmodified for vanilla blocks like leaves/sand, but our own blocks use these fields with our own mixin+tick-handler instead). Speed is read from FullStopCapability.getCurrentNativeVelocity() (vanilla blocks/tick scale). Omit this entire section for blocks with no phase behavior — this is the overwhelming majority of blocks.
_phaseable_note: When true, a getCollisionShape mixin (mirroring FullStop's own PhaseableBlockMixin, but keyed off this field) returns an empty collision shape for entities whose speed falls inside [phase_min_speed_mps, phase_max_speed_mps] — they pass through with zero collision. Below phase_min_speed_mps OR above phase_max_speed_mps, the block is normal solid collision either way — too slow to bother tunneling, too fast to trust tunneling through cleanly, so both ends of the range fail back to a wall.
_phase_min_note: m/s. Below this speed: solid collision (FullStop's own default threshold for leaves is 15.0)
_phase_max_note: m/s. Above this speed: solid collision. FullStop's original implementation has no ceiling at all — this is a Misanthrope-only addition. Pick a value comfortably above what a normal fast-moving entity/projectile should tunnel at, but below velocities you want to just slam into the block instead (e.g. a thrown item vs. a cannonball).
_phase_drag_note: Velocity multiplier applied per tick while an entity's bounding box overlaps this block and it's within the phase band. FullStop's own default for leaves is 0.9 (gentle bleed-off).
_engulfing_note: Implies phaseable behavior even if phaseable above is left false — an entity has to be able to enter the block volume before it can get stuck inside it. Uses phase_min_speed_mps/phase_max_speed_mps for the pass-through band unless engulf_min_speed_mps/engulf_max_speed_mps below are set, then applies engulf_drag_per_tick instead of phase_drag_per_tick while inside. Once the entity's speed drops back below phase_min_speed_mps it's now inside solid block geometry (collision resumes) and is stuck — same as FullStop's sand/gravel/snow, no separate 'escape' mechanic beyond breaking the block.
_engulf_drag_note: Heavier drag than phase_drag_per_tick — FullStop's own default for sand/gravel/snow is 0.5.
_engulf_particle_note: Spawn a block-particle 'burrowing' effect plus an occasional block-hit sound each tick while engulfed, matching FullStop's feel (its BlockPhasing.burrowEffects).
_engulf_speed_note: Optional override thresholds for engulfing specifically. null on either means fall back to phase_min_speed_mps/phase_max_speed_mps respectively. Leave both null unless a specific block needs a different speed band for engulfing than plain phasing would imply.
_ph_note: ph_value is always read (defaults to 7.0/neutral) — it's what the block IS. ph_reactivity is optional — it's how strongly the block RESPONDS to ambient pH that differs from ph_value. Omit ph_reactivity for chemically inert blocks (most blocks); set ph_value alone if you only need the block to register a non-neutral surface pH for some other system to read, without it corroding itself.
_ph_value_note: 0-14, 7.0 = neutral. Stone/concrete ~9-10 (alkaline, lime-bearing), most metals 7.0 (inert), organic/biological blocks ~5-6 (mildly acidic), limestone/marble ~9.5

ph_reactivity._exposure_note: Ambient exposure is read from MGE's EnvironmentGrid as a cumulative acid/base 'load', NOT a true pH average: load = sum over all gases present of (7.0 - gas_effective_ph) * partial_pressure_mbar. This is deliberately not an average — pH is a log scale, so a trace of a strong acid and a large volume of a weak acid can produce comparable load, which is the correct gameplay behaviour. Positive load = net acidic exposure; negative = net alkaline.
ph_reactivity._acid_rate_note: Strength-loss fraction accumulated per evaluation, per unit of positive (acidic) load. Reference: mild steel~0.00004, stainless/nickel~0.000006, copper/bronze~0.00003, limestone/marble~0.0002, cloth/fabric~0.0008, glass~0.00001 (glass resists acid)
ph_reactivity._base_rate_note: Strength-loss fraction per evaluation, per unit of negative (alkaline) load magnitude. Most materials resist base exposure far better than acid — default asymmetric. Glass is the notable exception (etched by base, not acid): glass~0.00015
ph_reactivity._resistance_note: Multiplier on BOTH rates above. 1.0 = baseline material, use rates as authored. Lower = more resistant (e.g. 0.05 for a corrosion-immune turbine fin alloy). This is the single field a 'resistant variant' of an otherwise-identical material should override — keep acid/base rates shared across a material family and only vary this
ph_reactivity._min_strength_note: Floor on strength fraction once corrosion accumulation reaches 1.0 (fully corroded) — mirrors how the thermal strength_retention_curve never quite reaches zero instantly. Default 0.05 (95% strength loss at full corrosion)
ph_reactivity._self_repair_note: Fractional self-repair of accumulated corrosion per evaluation (0-1). 0.0 = corrosion is PERMANENT (correct default — mirrors how structural cracks only grow or fail the block, never heal on their own). Non-zero = self-sealing surface, regenerating passivation layer, or magical ward. Use sparingly and intentionally

_stability_note: For exotic materials that require external fields or conditions to remain stable.
_te_note: Qualitative descriptor used by crack system. near_zero | low | moderate | high | extreme

radiation._type_note: alpha | beta | gamma | alpha_beta | gamma_beta | thermal_decay | void_energy | cherenkov_plus_radioluminescent | full_stellar_spectrum | multi_source | absorb_not_emit

_section_ousia: === ousia (loaded by ousia mod, ignored by BlockPhysicsRegistry) ===
_ousia_note: These fields are stored here for authoring convenience. BlockPhysicsRegistry silently ignores them. The ousia mod's own registry reads them from the same files.
